record.field: Typebot is a self-hostable, no-code builder for conversational chatbots and interactive forms.

Typebot is a self-hostable, no-code builder for conversational chatbots and interactive forms.

Using a drag-and-drop canvas, Typebot lets you compose conversation flows from dozens of building blocks, publish them as standalone pages or embed them on any website, and run them on your own infrastructure with full feature parity to the managed cloud. It targets marketers, product teams and developers who want engaging, conversational alternatives to static forms while keeping conversation data under their control.

Key features
  • Visual drag-and-drop flow editor with bubble, input and logic blocks
  • Conditional branching, JavaScript scripting, variables and A/B testing
  • Native integrations: OpenAI, Webhooks/HTTP, Google Sheets, Stripe, Zapier, Make and more
  • Embed anywhere (standalone, popup, bubble) plus WhatsApp and messaging channels
  • Built-in analytics and results collection for completed conversations
  • Self-hosted via Docker with PostgreSQL and Redis, no per-chat or per-bot pricing
Best for

Teams building lead-capture, onboarding, surveys, support deflection or interactive quizzes that want a conversational UX and on-premise data ownership instead of a SaaS form tool.

field.name: tag_ids
field.eval: [Command.set([ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_self_hosted'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_no_code'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_automation'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_workflow'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_communication'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_chat'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_analytics'), ref('agz_infra_suite.tag_ai')])]

record.field: #!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T typebot-db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d typebot-db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T typebot-db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T typebot-db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T typebot-db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
